broker_fee_detail: FARE Act notice shown; listed by building's broker (Supreme Plus Realty). Confirm no tenant-paid fee.
broker_message: Hi Harry, I'm interested in 1180 Ocean Parkway #6C. Could you confirm square footage, whether there's any tenant-paid or co-op fee, the board-approval timeline, and total move-in costs? Given a couple of similar studios nearby around $1,850, would the owner consider $1,850? I can move quickly for the right terms.
broker_questions: What is the square footage and can you send a floor plan?
Does the co-op board require approval, and how long?
Max sublet term and is renewal guaranteed?
How reliable is the single elevator?
Why are two units currently available?
building_risk_rationale: openigloo reports this 120-unit co-op at/below the NYC average on violations-per-unit, no bedbug reports in the past year, only minor recent items. Managing agent Miller Management LLC carries ~22 open violations across a ~58-unit portfolio — a modest yellow flag, not chronic neglect. Exact live open count behind paywall (unverified); direction favorable.
commute_notes: Q (Brighton) east and F (Culver) west both serve this stretch. Q express to Midtown ~45-55 min; F ~50-60 min. StreetEasy transit table is garbled — verify station/walk on a map before touring.
cons: No sqft/floor plan; studio 'separate kitchen' may be tight or windowless
Co-op sublet adds board approval and possible fees/renewal limits
Top floor of an aging building reliant on one elevator
leverage_notes: Priced mid-range vs comps ($1,850-$2,000); two-unit vacancy and generic studio (no sqft) give mild leverage. Fresh (2 days) so a modest ~$100 ask is realistic; don't overplay.
noise_risks: Ocean Parkway is a wide multi-lane arterial with a service road; front units may get traffic noise
plain_english: A fresh, fee-free, fairly priced Midwood studio in a clean-record elevator co-op. Main unknowns are size (no sqft) and co-op sublet mechanics. Only 2 days old so not sitting. Worth touring; confirm size and fees, and try a light $100 ask.
pros: No broker fee, priced at/below the local studio cluster
Elevator building with laundry and live-in super; clean building record
Good dual F/Q access on Ocean Parkway

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask for sqft and floor plan; measure on tour
red_flags.issue: Studio marketed as 'spacious'/'2 rooms' with no sqft
red_flags.why: No sqft or floor plan; 'separate kitchen area' can mean a windowless pass-through

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask about elevator reliability; check DOB elevator records
red_flags.issue: 6th-floor unit in a 6-story 1952 building — elevator dependency
red_flags.why: Single elevator in a 74-year-old building; outages strand a top-floor tenant

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask why both are open and how long each sat
red_flags.issue: Two units available in the same small co-op line
red_flags.why: Two vacancies can signal turnover (not alarming in 120 units)

red_flags.how_to_verify: Ask about board process, sublet fee, max term
red_flags.why: Board application, fees, and sublet-term limits

unverified: exact live HPD open violation count for 1180
